name: Empanadas
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Chopped black or green olives
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the butter into 1/4 inch cubes.
2. In a mixing bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, salt, and butter. Mix with a pastry cutter or steel blade in a food processor.
3. Beat the egg yolks. Mix half of them into the flour mixture. Reserve the remaining yolk for step 12.
4. Gradually incorporate water into the flour mixture until it becomes cohesive and semifirm mass.
5. Shape the dough into two balls. Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 15 minutes.
6. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
7. Heat oil in a large saute pan or skillet over moderate heat. Saute meat for 3 minutes, stirring frequently. Transfer meat to a warm bowl.
8. Add onions to the pan and saute over low to moderate heat for 3 minutes, stirring frequently. Add more oil if necessary.
9. Return meat to the pan and add remaining ingredients. Cook mixture for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Reserve for step 11.
10. Roll each dough ball into a 1/8-inch thick sheet on a lightly floured board. Cut rounds using a cookie cutter with a 4- to 5-inch diameter. Collect scraps and repeat until all dough is used.
11. Place one tablespoon of meat mixture in the center of each dough round. Lightly moisten exposed dough with cold tap water. Fold into half-moon shape, crimping edges to prevent stuffing from leaking during baking.
12. Brush tops of uncooked empanadas with reserved egg yolk.
13. Place uncooked empanadas on an ungreased baking sheet. Bake in the middle of the oven until tops are light golden brown (about 20 minutes). Serve immediately after removing from oven. Serves 4.
